description_long: This object is a double-headed sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular metal head on each end, both featuring a textured, grid-like striking surface. The central section of the head is reinforced with a prominent bolt or rivet, suggesting durability and secure attachment to the handle. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of a reddish-brown material, possibly wood or a composite, with a slightly darker grip at the base for improved handling. The hammer's overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, focusing on function and strength. The two hammers shown are of different sizes, likely representing a standard and a smaller variant, both sharing identical construction and features. The surfaces are clean and free from visible wear, indicating the hammers are either new or well-maintained.
description: A double-headed sledgehammer with a textured metal head and a straight, reddish-brown handle, shown in two different sizes. Both hammers have robust, grid-patterned striking faces and a clean, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Two double-headed sledgehammers are shown side by side, highlighting their size difference and the full length of their straight handles.
description_view_1: The hammers are viewed from above, clearly displaying the grid-patterned striking faces on both ends of each head.
description_view_2: The hammers are rotated to show the opposite side, again emphasizing the size difference and the symmetry of the double-headed design.
description_view_3: A direct end-on view of the hammers, focusing on the textured striking face and the alignment of the handle beneath the head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood or composite, reddish-brown in color with a slightly darker grip at the base, smooth with minimal texturing.
attribute_head_type: Double-headed sledgehammer type with rectangular steel heads, both ends featuring a grid-patterned striking face for enhanced grip and impact.
